summary: Tested all 4 bug fixes: 1) Dynamic DINE_DIMS text per pet (getDineDims function), 2) Admin Dine Products category filter fix, 3) Mira's Picks Salmon products for Mojo, 4) ServiceBox.jsx compile error (missing catch clause - FIXED). Also fixed a compile error in ServiceBox.jsx that was blocking the frontend.

ui_bugs.issue: Lola shows 'Allergy: No' in the hero and Mira's quote says 'I've already removed everything containing No. What you see is safe.' - because Lola's doggy_soul_answers has 'No' stored as an allergy value. The CLEAN_NONE regex should filter it but the value might be stored differently. This causes confusing UI text for pet owners.

Daily Meals card shows 'Salmon treats-forward' instead of pure 'Salmon-forward' because getLoves(pet) returns 'Salmon treats' (from favorite_treats) as the first element. The treats card then shows 'Salmon treats treats first — Mojo loves these' (double 'treats'). Technically correct per data but awkward phrasing.

test_results_detail.dynamic_dims_mojo: PASS — Mojo shows 'Salmon treats-forward, chicken-free, right for Mojo' (dynamic, not hardcoded Salmon)
test_results_detail.dynamic_dims_lola: PASS — Lola shows 'Chicken-forward, allergy-checked, right for Lola' (NOT 'Salmon-forward')
test_results_detail.badge_mojo_body_needs: PASS — 'Mojo's body needs this' badge showing correctly
test_results_detail.badge_mojo_loves_these: PASS — 'Mojo loves these' badge showing correctly
test_results_detail.badge_lola_body_needs: PASS — 'Lola's body needs this' badge showing correctly
test_results_detail.badge_lola_loves_these: PASS — 'Lola loves these' badge showing correctly
test_results_detail.admin_filter_initial: PASS — Initial load shows 594 products (All Categories)
test_results_detail.admin_filter_homemade_recipes: PASS — Selecting 'Homemade & Recipes' shows exactly 7 products (Birthday Cake Ingredient Pack, DIY Salmon Birthday Cake Recipe, Doggy Safe Ingredients Reference Guide, Peanut Butter Frozen Treats Recipe, Salmon & Oat Ingredient Pack, Salmon & Sweet Potato Biscuit Recipe, Slow-Cooked Lamb Stew Recipe). NOT Akita products.
test_results_detail.admin_filter_no_akita: PASS — No Akita products showing with Homemade & Recipes filter
test_results_detail.miras_picks_mojo_salmon: PASS — Mira's Picks shows Salmon Training Bites, Salmon & Lentil Evening Dinner, Salmon & Sweet Potato Morning Bowl, Salmon-Only Biscuits, Fresh Cooked Salmon Weekly Box, Salmon Oil, Salmon Biscuit Box etc.
test_results_detail.miras_picks_no_akita: PASS — No Akita products in Mira's Picks for Mojo
test_results_detail.servicebox_compile_error: FIXED — ServiceBox.jsx had missing catch clause (line 1054) for XHR-based AI image generation. Added catch block.

action_items: LOW: Fix Lola's 'No' allergy being displayed — check if doggy_soul_answers.food_allergies is storing 'No' string. The CLEAN_NONE regex should handle /^no$/i but might not match if there's extra whitespace or different casing. Fix: trim and lowercase before regex test in getAllergies(), or clean stored data in DB

LOW: Fix double 'treats' text — 'Salmon treats treats first' — the treats card template adds 'treats first' to favProtein which is already 'Salmon treats'. Fix: strip 'treats/treat' from the end of favProtein before applying to treats template
critical_code_review_comments: ServiceBox.jsx (FIXED): XHR async block at line 1054 had no catch clause. Fixed by adding: '} catch (err) { setSaving(false); toast({ title: Error, description: err.message, variant: destructive }); }'

DineSoulPage.jsx: getLoves() adds pet?.doggy_soul_answers?.favorite_treats first, then favorite_protein. For Mojo, favorite_treats='Salmon treats' so favProtein='Salmon treats' leading to 'Salmon treats-forward'. This is functionally correct but could be improved to extract the protein from the treat name.

DineSoulPage.jsx: getAllergies() uses CLEAN_NONE = /^(no|none|none_confirmed|no_allergies|no allergies)$/i but Lola has 'No' stored somewhere in soul answers. The filter should work for 'No' but may not be catching it from all paths.

success_rate.frontend: 95% — All main bug fixes verified. 2 minor LOW priority text issues found.

test_credentials: Admin: aditya / lola4304 at /admin. User: dipali@clubconcierge.in / test123 (Mojo pet active). Lola pet ID: pet-lola-0faaab37
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 bug fixes verified working: 1) getDineDims(pet) correctly uses pet-specific data for dimension cards (Lola shows Chicken-forward, Mojo shows Salmon treats-forward). 2) Admin Dine Products filter now correctly shows 7 Homemade & Recipes products when that category is selected (not 594 Akita products). 3) Mira's Picks for Mojo shows Salmon products, no Akita. ServiceBox.jsx compile error fixed (missing catch in XHR block). Lola pet access: set localStorage.setItem('selectedPetId', 'pet-lola-0faaab37') then navigate to /dine.
rca_of_issues: 1) getDineDims hardcoded bug: was using global DINE_DIMS constant with hardcoded Salmon text. Fixed by converting to getDineDims(pet) function that calls getLoves(pet) for dynamic protein. 2) Admin filter stale closure: fetchProducts was using captured filterCategory from closure. Fixed by adding catOverride param and passing new value directly from onChange. 3) ServiceBox.jsx: XHR XMLHttpRequest code inside onClick had a try block without catch clause (syntax error). Fixed by adding catch.
